posts.headline: DOJ files civil forfeiture to seize Mansour Barzani's 30 million dollar Beverly Hills mansion
posts.summary: The US Justice Department filed a civil forfeiture complaint this week to seize Mansour Barzani's Beverly Hills mansion, saying it was bought and renovated with 30 million dollars tied to a bribery scheme around US military fuel contracts during the anti-ISIS campaign.

posts.headline: US DOJ posts 10 million dollar reward for information on Kata'ib Sayyid al-Shuhada leader Abu Ala al-Walai
posts.summary: The US Department of Justice announced a 10 million dollar reward for any information on Abu Ala al-Walai, the head of Kata'ib Sayyid al-Shuhada, as Washington tightens pressure on Iran-aligned Iraqi factions through the PM formation window. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Iranian drones strike areas inside Erbil city
posts.summary: The account reports Iranian drone attacks on areas inside Erbil city on Thursday evening, consistent with the wave of strikes on the Darashakran corridor and Coalition jet intercepts reported by Rudaw the same night. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Israeli alert and US messaging flagged in early-evening cable
posts.summary: Breaking note on Israeli alert posture and parallel US messaging into the region, framed as a shift in both sides' signalling during the extended Iran ceasefire. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Trump remarks on Iran political dynamics and US posture on the Strait of Hormuz
posts.summary: The account summarises Trump's statements on Iran's internal political situation and on US control over the Strait of Hormuz during the ceasefire, landing as Hormuz tanker traffic remains near zero. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Parliamentary delegation to travel to Erbil as Coordination Framework deadlock drags on
posts.summary: A source to Al-Sabi'a says a parliamentary delegation is heading to Erbil as part of the consultative track around the PM file, with the Coordination Framework still unable to agree on Badry's quorum path before the April 27 deadline. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: US embassy in Baghdad reinforces its defensive systems
posts.summary: The US embassy compound in Baghdad has upgraded its defensive systems over recent days, tracking with US warnings that Iran-backed militias could escalate during the PM formation window. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Speaker and political bloc heads agree to extend the current legislative session
posts.summary: Parliament leadership and bloc chiefs agreed to extend the current legislative session, keeping the chamber available for a confirmation vote or for any supplementary fiscal legislation if the cabinet process slips into May. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Oil Price investigation flags structural shift inside Iraq's armed-faction landscape
posts.summary: An OilPrice.com investigation, shared by the account, argues a structural reshaping is underway inside Iraq's Shia armed factions that changes both the escalation menu and the economic interfaces around oil and fuel logistics. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: US Department of War says forces boarded a vessel the previous day
posts.summary: The US Department of War confirmed its forces boarded a vessel in the preceding 24 hours, the latest datapoint in the tit-for-tat ship seizures between US and Iranian units inside the ostensible ceasefire. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Luay al-Khatib warns the squeeze could prove worse than the 1990s sanctions siege
posts.summary: Former electricity minister Luay al-Khatib says the combined hit from Hormuz disruption and stalled oil flows could bite Iraqi public finances harder than the 1990s siege if the Coordination Framework cannot close the PM file. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Baghdad parallel dollar rate widens to 156,500 dinars per 100 dollar note
posts.summary: The account flags a fresh uptick in the parallel dinar rate, with the 100 dollar note trading at 156,500 Iraqi dinars (about 1,565 IQD per USD), well above the CBI's official 1,300 and bank-window 1,310 tiers. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Lebanon-Israel meeting relocated from State Department to the White House
posts.summary: Retweet flags that today's Lebanon-Israel meeting was moved from the State Department to the White House, with the possibility Trump himself stops by, a meaningful upgrade of the diplomatic ceiling on the track.

posts.headline: Saudi-Lebanon engagement stacks up, MBS-Aoun call, Berri-Prince Faisal call, envoy to Beirut, Berri aide to Riyadh
posts.summary: Retweet notes an intensified Saudi-Lebanon engagement cycle in the past week, with MBS-Aoun and Berri-Prince Faisal phone calls, the Saudi envoy for Lebanon visiting Beirut, and a top Berri aide travelling to Riyadh.

posts.headline: Argus Media 'Strait Talk' episode frames the Hormuz truce as truce-or-dare
posts.summary: Retweet of the Argus Media podcast interrogating whether the Hormuz ceasefire is holding or simply being ridden out, useful for readers tracking tanker traffic and insurance premia inside the ceasefire window.

posts.headline: Oman Energy Ministry denies reports of oil and gas discoveries by PetroTel Energy
posts.summary: Retweet of the Oman Ministry of Energy and Minerals denying circulating reports that PetroTel Energy had made material oil and gas discoveries inside the sultanate, a clarification that matters for Gulf upstream comparables. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: German Chancellor welcomes US-Iran ceasefire extension as opening for diplomacy
posts.summary: Retweet of the German federal chancellor welcoming the extension of the US-Iran ceasefire and urging its use to resume formal negotiations, the clearest European readout on the extension yet.

posts.headline: Japan PM Takaichi asks MBS to expand Saudi crude supplies on bilateral call
posts.summary: Bacharel Halabi reports Japanese Prime Minister Takaichi Sanae asked Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman to expand Saudi crude volumes to Japan during a call, a direct ask as Gulf supply sits under Hormuz-driven stress.

posts.headline: QatarEnergy celebrates first LNG export cargo from the 18 mtpa Golden Pass project in Texas
posts.summary: QatarEnergy published imagery marking the first LNG export cargo from its 18 million tonne per year Golden Pass facility in Texas, a meaningful US supply addition at a moment of peak concern over Gulf shipping.

posts.headline: Knight argues the contrast with Earnest Will and Desert Shield could not be sharper
posts.summary: Knight contrasts the current hesitant US naval tempo in the Gulf with Operation Earnest Will and Desert Shield, framing the limited response as a departure from the reassurance posture Gulf partners have historically expected from Washington.

posts.headline: First US fast-air movement back into the Gulf states since February
posts.summary: Retweet flagging the first US tactical-air rotation back into Gulf partner bases since the late-February drawdown, a visible signal that Washington is restoring regional posture even as the ceasefire drags on.

posts.headline: US ramps up pressure on Iraq to select a PM and form a government
posts.summary: Retweet amplifying reporting that US pressure on Iraq to name a PM and seat a cabinet is intensifying as the April 27 constitutional deadline approaches and the Coordination Framework remains split on quorum.

posts.headline: Araji says Sudani is modelling his office on the Putin playbook by nominating his chief of staff
posts.summary: Retweet of 964arabic summarising senior politician Araji's claim that Prime Minister Sudani is modelled on the Russian template and has floated his own chief of staff for the premiership, a pointed commentary on the current PM file. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Knight reads the current moves as the early shape of a Hormuz 'super Freedom of Navigation' effort
posts.summary: Knight argues that the combination of ship seizures, US air movement, and allied signalling looks like the early form of a much larger Freedom of Navigation effort in and around Hormuz rather than a narrow tit-for-tat.

posts.headline: Knight says the word Gulf states should want from China is sodium perchlorate
posts.summary: Knight argues Gulf state asks of China should be concrete and named, pointing to sodium perchlorate, a key solid-fuel precursor, as the bargaining chip that actually moves Iranian missile capability rather than vague diplomatic platitudes.
